questions.question: The talks ___ lead to an agreement – both sides seem willing.
She ___ not attend the conference – she hasn't decided yet.
It's ___ that he will resign by the end of the month.
They ___ open a new branch next year if sales improve.
He ___ probably not remember – it was years ago.
There's a strong ___ they will merge the two departments.
She ___ be selected – she's one of the top candidates.
It ___ take up to three weeks to process your application.
I doubt he ___ change his mind at this stage.
The project is ___ to be finished ahead of schedule.
We ___ go to Italy this summer – nothing is booked yet.
He's ___ to know about the changes – he's very well-informed.
She ___ not get the visa in time – the process is slow.
There ___ be a strike next week if the talks fail.
I'm not sure – I ___ stay or I ___ go home early.
The weather ___ improve by the weekend.
___ is a chance that the event will be cancelled.
He ___ call you back – he said he would try.
It's ___ impossible to finish all this by noon.
She ___ decide to quit – she's very unhappy there.
